titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/117

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=387 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:35:10.310206+00 | translation moa=388 status=live modified=2021-04-06 13:35:14.700958+00

div.p:
W. dei gratia Rex Scottorum . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Mi
nistris . et omnibus probis hominibus totius terre sue . Salutem . Sciant presentes et posteri me dedisse . et
hac mea carta confirmasse Deo et Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros et Monachos ibidem deo seruien
tibus illam terram quam tenent in territorio de Cliftuna per ipsas diuisas que continentur in
carta Walteri de Windlesoure . quam habent de eo de Eadem terra . ita ut quicquid infra eas
dem diuisas fuerit . habeant et possideant in perpetuum . ita libere et quiete . honorifice et pacifi
ce  sicut aliqua elemosina liberius . quietius . et honorificentius in regno meo possident . Dedi enim
eis et confirmaui eam liberam ab omnibus auxiliis . placitis . Geldis . Scutagiis . Cornagiis . et ab omni seruitio et Consuetudine et Exactione seculari . ita quod ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et
Warantizabimus eam illis Contra omnes homines in perpetuum . saluo seruitio meo de Waltero et her
edibus suis . Teste dauid fratre meo . Comite Dunecano . Comite Gilberto . Willelmo de lindesaie . Rogero
et Philippo de Valoniis .Waltero de Berchel' . Iohanne de londoniis . APVD . PERT

William by the grace of God king of the Scots to the Bishops, Abbots, Earls, Barons, Iustices, Sheriffs, Provosts, Officials, and all the worthy men of the whole of his land sends greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the Church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the land they hold in the territory of Clifton by the same boundaries contained in Walter of Windsor's charter which they have from him about the same land; so that they should have and possess for ever whatever is within the same boundaries just as freely and quitely, honourably and peaceably as they possess any alms in my kingdom freely, quitely and honourably. I have indeed given and confirmed it to them free from all aids, pleas, gelds, scutages, cornages, and from every secular service, custom, and exaction, so that I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant it to them against all men for ever; except for my service from Walter and his heirs. As witness: David, my brother; Earl Duncan; Earl Gilbert; William of Lindsay; Roger and Philip de Valognes; Walter of Berkeley; John of London. AT PERTH.
